passions_detected.false_judgement: That children's disobedience is a genuine evil requiring forceful correction
That being instructed differently is an insult to competence
That betrayal by a friend is evidence of personal worthlessness
That reputation in friend group defines self-worth

causal_observations.description: Disappointment in kids' current behaviour leads to assent that it will become exponentially worse — catastrophic extrapolation from impression.
Push-back against instructions leads to expressed dissatisfaction, damaging team morale — impulse not governed by reason.
Betrayal experience: assented to the impression that being sold out means personal worthlessness rather than recognising addiction's role.

value_observations.observed_as: still clung to — 'zero contradictions' and 'my way is the right way'
genuine wrestling — lists concrete areas needing acceptance

virtue_observations.evidence: Recognises overreaction patterns. Beginning to practice acceptance of imperfection.
Can rationally analyse the betrayal situation — 'addictions override decency, it was not personal' — but emotional residue persists.
Lists 18 things to change about self — honest self-examination including dark thoughts, temper, cynicism.

key_passages.summary: Betrayal by drug-addicted friend — heartbroken, worthless, sold out for a few thousand dollars. Rationally understands addiction but emotionally unresolved.
Lists 18 character traits to change with control assessment — remarkable self-awareness including cynicism, negativity, dark thoughts, oppositional tendency
